115.code: for(p = G[i].link; p != NULL; pri = p, p = p->next)
115.reason: The loop update 'p = p->next' executes after 'p' is freed inside the loop, causing use-after-free. Also, 'i' is incremented inside this loop, incorrectly advancing the vertex index per edge.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The loop update 'p = p->next' executes after 'p' is freed inside the loop, causing use-after-free. Also, 'i' is incremented inside this loop, incorrectly advancing the vertex index per edge.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  'pri' is uninitialized on the first iteration. Also, freeing 'p' inside the loop and then accessing 'p->next' in the loop increment causes undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop condition and update are flawed: 'pri' is used uninitialized in the first iteration if G[i].link is not NULL. Also, 'free(p)' inside the loop causes use-after-free when accessing p->next.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If G[i].link is NULL, the loop won't run, but 'i' is never incremented in that case, causing infinite loop or skipped vertices]
